audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for mercy-giving, prayer, secrecy, Father language, Lord's Prayer, debts, fasting, treasures, eye/lamp imagery, Mammon, worry, birds/lilies, and seeking the reign and righteousness/justice.

verses.restored: Pay attention not to do your righteousness/justice before people in order to be seen by them; otherwise you have no reward with your Father in the heavens.

Therefore when you give mercy-gifts, do not announce it dramatically as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and streets, so they may be honored by people. Amen, I say to you: they have received their reward.
But when you give mercy-gifts, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,
so that your mercy-gift may be in secret; and your Father who sees in secret will give back to you.

When you pray, you shall not be like the hypocrites, because they love to stand and pray in the synagogues and at the street corners so they may appear to people. Amen, I say to you: they have received their reward.
But you, when you pray, enter your inner room, shut your door, and pray to your Father in secret; and your Father who sees in secret will give back to you.
When praying, do not babble on like the nations, for they think they will be heard by their many words.
Therefore do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask him.
Therefore pray this way: Our Father in the heavens, let your name be made holy.
Let your reign come; let your will come to be, as in heaven, also on earth.
Give us today our bread for the coming day / our needed bread.
And release us from our debts, as we also have released our debtors.
And do not bring us into testing, but rescue us from the harmful one.
For if you release people from their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also release you.
But if you do not release people, neither will your Father release your trespasses.
When you fast, do not become gloomy-faced like the hypocrites, for they alter their faces so they may appear to people as fasting. Amen, I say to you: they have received their reward.
But you, when fasting, anoint your head and wash your face,
so that you may not appear to people as fasting, but to your Father in secret; and your Father who sees in secret will give back to you.
Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moth and decay consume and where thieves break in and take.
But store up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor decay consumes and where thieves do not break in or take.
For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
The lamp of the body is the eye. Therefore if your eye is single/clear/generous, your whole body will be full of light.
But if your eye is harmful, your whole body will be dark. Therefore if the light in you is darkness, how great is that darkness!
No one is able to serve two masters; for either he will reject the one and love the other, or hold to one and despise the other. You cannot serve the Divine and Mammon.
Because of this I say to you: do not be worried for your life, what you will eat or drink, nor for your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothing?
Look at the birds of the heavens: they do not sow, reap, or gather into barns, and your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not worth more than they?
Which of you by worrying is able to add one cubit/span to his life?
And why are you worried about clothing? Learn from the lilies of the field, how they grow; they do not labor or spin.
Yet I say to you that not even Solomon in all his glory was clothed like one of these.
If the Divine clothes the grass of the field this way, alive today and tomorrow placed in the oven, will he not much more clothe you, ones of little trust?
Therefore do not be worried, saying, What shall we eat? or What shall we drink? or What shall we wear?
For the nations seek after all these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them all.
But seek first the reign and righteousness/justice of the Divine, and all these things will be added to you.
Therefore do not be worried for tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. Sufficient for the day is its own trouble.
